PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for (nominated executor) Charles Barnhouse.

Ms. Young reported that there have been no objections, that copies of the Raifsnider declaration had been sent out, that the two brothers are deceased and that their children have been given notice. The court stated that there was sufficient evidence to overcome that the will was destroyed and granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary, signing the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Continued To Mar-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Continued To Mar-04-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for (nominated executor) Charles Barnhouse.

The court referred to time records filed with the conservatorship which indicated that the conservatee's former counsel Mr. Raifsnider had a series of telephone conservations and correspondence in 2005 labeled as discussions regarding the will and stated that we do not know what these discussions were. Ms. Young stated they have a will that was never signed or dated, that it named two other possible heirs, that they have been given notice and that no one has objected. The court stated that it needs to have sufficient evidence to overcome the presumption that absent the will the conservatee had any intent to destroy it. The court directed Ms. Young to have conservation with Mr. Raifsnider regarding discussions that took place regarding the will. The petition for probate of lost will, etc. was continued to March 4, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Young to file a declaration regarding said discussions.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-31-2014 Continued To Jan-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for Charles Barnhouse.

Ms. Young stated that she spoke with Ms. Rasch (conservator's attorney) about their knowledge concerning the decedent's will, that Ms. Rasch knew nothing about the will, that she has received no replies, spoke of family dynamics and stated that there is nothing suggesting that there is another will. The petition for probate of will and letters testamentary was continued to January 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a declaration from the conservator regarding her thoughts on the decedent's will and the decedent's intent.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Continued To Dec-31-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Continued To Dec-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for and with Charles Barnhouse.

Ms. Young stated that is was the family's understanding that this was the decedent's original will and that other family members have been advised of this proceeding. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to December 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide a copy of the petition and notice to the family members.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
